page: Amazon Web Services m5.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8175M processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs, which map to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the m5.large in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute performance. Its single-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier, while multi-core CPU metrics and Passmark scores rank in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show a similar trend, with large block read and write speeds of 13.29 GB/sec and 11.49 GB/sec respectively, both placing in the bottom 10%. The instance also demonstrates limited throughput in database operations, Redis SET operations at 1.65 million operations per second, and static web serving at 1.94 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5.large offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a standard entry-level option for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation rather than burstable performance. However, due to its lower relative benchmark scores, it is best suited for low-intensity applications. Appropriate workloads include small-scale web serving, light development or testing environments, basic background workers, and low-traffic database instances that do not require high IOPS or intensive memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.large is an x86_64 general-purpose instance running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core with hyper-threading) clocked at 3.1 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate that the m5.large sits in the lower performance tiers, with single-core and multi-core metrics ranking in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth and database operations also show entry-level performance. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for small-scale web serving, development environments, and lightweight background tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8175M processor, designed for entry-level dedicated workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual instance offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing and balanced memory for entry-level workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for low-intensity workloads, development environments, and small web servers
